rationale: The cited paper is used in a specific methodological role, not just as general background. In the measurement section, the citing paper explicitly invokes Kornish and Ulrich (2014) to support using consumer judges (rather than experts) to assess idea quality/innovativeness and to justify that small consumer panels can be informative. This directly supports how the citing study operationalizes and defends its dependent variable, even though it is not central to the network-clustering theory.
relation_description: The citing paper applies an empirical insight from the cited paper - that consumer evaluations can be strong predictors of idea quality - to justify its own consumer-based idea-innovativeness judging procedure. The citation functions as methodological support for measurement validity in a different ideation context.
